pattern: Since every character of the same letter always transforms identically, tracking how many characters of each of the 26 letters exist is enough -- the actual string never needs to be rebuilt.
hints: You never need the literal resulting string, only its length, and every occurrence of the same letter transforms the same way, so track a length-26 count of how many characters currently are each letter instead of the string itself.

One transformation step moves each letter's count forward to the next letter, except 'z', whose count splits and lands on BOTH 'a' and 'b' -- so each step is just redistributing 26 counters based on fixed rules.
Repeat that count-redistribution exactly t times (t is small enough to simulate step by step), then sum all 26 counts modulo 1e9+7 for the final answer.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a 'z' splits into two letters partway through
statement example, single transformation with one wraparound
t at its smallest legal value
every letter is 'z', so length doubles each transformation
letter one step away from wrapping, exercises the boundary between shift and split

stress.description: long random string with many transformations, exercising the modulo arithmetic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(n))
    t = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [s, t]
